---
title: "Impact"
sidebar: false
page-layout: article
toc: false
title-block-banner: true
---

## Overview

Supported by a [Mozilla Responsible Computing Challenge](https://foundation.mozilla.org/en/blog/mozilla-announces-15-new-responsible-computing-challenge-awardees-in-the-us/) grant, **Responsible Datasets in Context** has grown into a widely adopted resource used by instructors, students, librarians, and researchers across disciplines and institutions.

## By the Numbers

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-4 style="text-align: center; padding: 1rem;"}
### 20,000+
unique website visitors
:::

::: {.g-col-4 style="text-align: center; padding: 1rem;"}
### 500+
students who have worked with our materials in a class setting
:::

::: {.g-col-4 style="text-align: center; padding: 1rem;"}
### 10+
university library guides
:::

::: {.g-col-4 style="text-align: center; padding: 1rem;"}
### 7+
institutions with courses using our materials
:::

:::

## Awards & Recognition

- [**Digital Project Award**](https://www.theasa.net/about/news-events/announcements/press-release-immediate-release-american-studies-association)—American Studies Association --- awarded to Responsible Datasets in Context for its contribution to digital scholarship in American Studies.

- [**POEM (Project on Open and Evolving Metaliteracies)**](https://library.cmu.edu/poem) --- forthcoming in this IMLS-funded open educational resource collection at Carnegie Mellon University.

- [**Big Ten Data Visualization Championship**](https://publish.illinois.edu/lovedataweek/love-data-week-2025/) (Love Data Week 2025) --- our National Park visit dataset was selected as the featured dataset for this multi-institution data visualization competition.

<!-- ## Student Impact

Students who have engaged with our materials report that they transform how they think about data:

> "I had thought about the politics involved with data before, but I feel like this is the first time I was explicitly examining specific examples of politics of data collection, and I found it genuinely interesting. I can definitely see myself thinking about these ideas the next time I'm looking at a dataset."
>
> --- Student reflecting on NPS visit data and the politics of data collection

> "I find it very interesting how the formation of a 'National Park' could lead to the displacement of the Native peoples who originally occupied the land."
>
> --- Student reflecting on Indigenous land displacement and the National Parks

> "I didn't know about the Wayback Machine, but it's actually amazing that there's a massive archive of stuff from the internet."
>
> --- Student discovering digital archiving through course exercises -->

## Press, Media, & Citations

- ["Trump Administration Bars NPS from Reporting Visitor Data"](https://mountainjournal.org/trump-administration-bars-national-park-service-from-reporting-visitation-data/) --- *Mountain Journal*
- ["Miriam Posner Awarded Mozilla Grant"](https://seis.ucla.edu/news/information-studies-scholar-miriam-posner-is-part-of-team-awarded-mozilla-foundation-grant/) --- *UCLA School of Education & Information Studies*
- [**Big Ten Data Visualization Championship**](https://publish.illinois.edu/lovedataweek/love-data-week-2025/) --- *University of Illinois Siebel School of Computing and Data Science*
- Cited in:
    - ["Provocations from the Humanities for Generative AI"](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.19190) --- Klein et al.
    - ["Design Studio as Method: Reparative Archives and Beyond"](https://doi.org/10.3998/jep.6168) --- Lindblad, Grumbach, & Boyles (*Journal of Electronic Publishing*)
    - ["Border Women Literature and Feminist Cartographies"](https://doi.org/10.1002/sgp2.70025) --- Esmaeili, Corona, & Medrano (*Signs: Gender and Policy*)
    - [Combatting Bias](https://combattingbias.huygens.knaw.nl/) toolkit --- Huygens Institute / Dutch Research Council
    - ["Bias in AI: How to Spot It, Why It Matters, and What You Can Do"](https://www.library.jhu.edu/news/2025/09/bias-in-ai-how-to-spot-it-why-it-matters-and-what-you-can-do/) --- McKone (*Johns Hopkins University Libraries*)

## Presentations

- **Digital Humanities 2024** (DH2024)
- **Modern Language Association 2025** (MLA 2025)
- **Data Advocacy for All**, CU Boulder 2025
- **University of Texas at San Antonio**
- **UW Open Scholarship Commons**: "Situating Data: Strategies for Curation and Contextualization"

## Student Projects

Our datasets have inspired independent student projects and creative work:

- **Big Ten Love Data Week** --- students created data visualizations using our National Park dataset as part of the multi-institution [data visualization competition](https://publish.illinois.edu/lovedataweek/love-data-week-2025/).
- Student-driven analyses published on [RPubs](https://rpubs.com/) using R and our datasets.
